Operation 'GetTemplateVersion' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. The summary is clear and action-oriented, but the description lacks critical details about what parameters are required (template_id, version_id), what the response contains, and the use case context. Added specificity about required identifiers and response content to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_summary: Retrieve a specific transactional template version.
data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to retrieve a specific version of a template.**
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a specific version of a transactional template by template ID and version ID. Returns the complete template version object including content, metadata, creation date, and editor information. This operation is useful for reviewing template version details before activation or modification.
data.reasoning: The summary is clear and action-oriented, but the description lacks critical details about what parameters are required (template_id, version_id), what the response contains, and the use case context. Added specificity about required identifiers and response content to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'UpdateTemplateVersion' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The summary is adequate, but the description is too vague about what can be edited and what the operation returns. Added clarity about supported fields, partial update capability, return value, and important side effect information (that updates don't automatically activate the version) to help AI agents understand the operation's scope and limitations.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to edit the content of your template version.**
data.description_suggestion: Updates the content and metadata of a specific transactional template version. Accepts partial updates to template properties such as subject, HTML content, plain text content, and other version attributes. Returns the updated template version object. Note: Updating a version does not affect the active status; use the activate endpoint to set a version as active.
data.reasoning: The summary is adequate, but the description is too vague about what can be edited and what the operation returns. Added clarity about supported fields, partial update capability, return value, and important side effect information (that updates don't automatically activate the version) to help AI agents understand the operation's scope and limitations.

message: Operation 'DeleteTemplateVersion' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, missing_context. The summary is clear, but the description lacks important context about the destructive nature of the operation, what happens if the active version is deleted, and the response format. Added critical side effect information and clarification about the response to help AI agents understand the consequences of this operation.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to delete a transactional template version.**
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a specific version of a transactional template. The version is identified by template ID and version ID. Deletion is irreversible. If the deleted version is currently active, no version will be active until another version is explicitly activated. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: The summary is clear, but the description lacks important context about the destructive nature of the operation, what happens if the active version is deleted, and the response format. Added critical side effect information and clarification about the response to help AI agents understand the consequences of this operation.

message: Operation 'ActivateTemplateVersion' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, missing_context. The summary is clear, but the description lacks crucial operational context about what 'activate' means in the SendGrid system (it becomes the version used in Mail Send API), the constraint that only one version can be active, and the side effect of deactivating the previous version. Added this context to help AI agents understand the operation's significance and impact on template behavior.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to activate a version of one of your templates.**
data.description_suggestion: Sets a specific template version as the active version for a transactional template. When a template version is activated, it becomes the version that will be used when the template is referenced by ID in SendGrid's Mail Send API. Only one version per template can be active at a time; activating a new version automatically deactivates the previously active version. Returns the activated template version object.
data.reasoning: The summary is clear, but the description lacks crucial operational context about what 'activate' means in the SendGrid system (it becomes the version used in Mail Send API), the constraint that only one version can be active, and the side effect of deactivating the previous version. Added this context to help AI agents understand the operation's significance and impact on template behavior.

message: Operation 'CreateTemplate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is minimal and lacks critical details. Added information about template purpose, input requirements (template name), output (template ID), account-level scope, and the 300-template limit to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to create a transactional template.**
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new transactional template for your account. The template serves as a reusable container for email content that can have multiple versions. Requires a template name in the request body. Returns the created template object with a unique template ID that can be used in subsequent API calls. Templates are account-specific and not accessible to Subusers. Each account can create up to 300 templates.
data.reasoning: Original description is minimal and lacks critical details. Added information about template purpose, input requirements (template name), output (template ID), account-level scope, and the 300-template limit to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'ListTemplate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original description doesn't explain pagination support or what data is returned. Enhanced to clarify pagination capability, return structure, and use cases for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to retrieve all transactional templates.**
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all transactional templates for your account. Supports filtering and pagination parameters to control the results returned. Returns an array of template objects with metadata including template IDs, names, creation dates, and version information. Useful for discovering available templates or building template management interfaces.
data.reasoning: Original description doesn't explain pagination support or what data is returned. Enhanced to clarify pagination capability, return structure, and use cases for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'DuplicateTemplate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specifics about what gets duplicated (versions included), input requirements, and return value. Added clarity on scope of duplication, parameter requirements, and practical use cases.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to duplicate a transactional template.**
data.description_suggestion: Creates a copy of an existing transactional template, including all its versions. Requires the source template ID as a path parameter. The duplicated template receives a new unique template ID and can be customized independently. Returns the newly created template object. Useful for creating template variants or backing up templates before modifications.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specifics about what gets duplicated (versions included), input requirements, and return value. Added clarity on scope of duplication, parameter requirements, and practical use cases.

message: Operation 'GetTemplate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Original description is too minimal. Added information about what data is returned and practical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to retrieve a single transactional template.**
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information about a specific transactional template by its template ID. Returns the template object including name, creation metadata, and associated version information. Use this operation to inspect template details or verify template configuration before sending emails.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added information about what data is returned and practical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'UpdateTemplate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary says 'Edit' which is vague about scope. Enhanced summary to clarify it's metadata-only. Description now explicitly states what can be updated (name only), input requirements, return value, and importantly clarifies the distinction between updating metadata vs. content to prevent AI agent confusion.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to edit the name of a transactional template.**

To edit the template itself, [create a new transactional template version](https://docs.sendgrid.com/api-reference/transactional-templates-versions/create-a-new-transactional-template-version).
data.description_suggestion: Updates the metadata of a transactional template, specifically the template name. Requires the template ID as a path parameter and the new name in the request body. Returns the updated template object. Note: To modify template content (subject, HTML, plain text), create a new template version instead using the CreateTemplateVersion operation. This operation only affects template properties, not its content.
data.reasoning: Original summary says 'Edit' which is vague about scope. Enhanced summary to clarify it's metadata-only. Description now explicitly states what can be updated (name only), input requirements, return value, and importantly clarifies the distinction between updating metadata vs. content to prevent AI agent confusion.

message: Operation 'DeleteTemplate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks critical information about permanence and scope (all versions deleted). Added warnings about irreversibility, side effects (template ID becomes unusable), and clarified return behavior for AI agent safety and understanding.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to delete a transactional template.**
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a transactional template and all of its associated versions. Requires the template ID as a path parameter. This action cannot be undone. After deletion, the template ID cannot be used in sending API calls. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks critical information about permanence and scope (all versions deleted). Added warnings about irreversibility, side effects (template ID becomes unusable), and clarified return behavior for AI agent safety and understanding.

message: Operation 'CreateTemplateVersion' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too generic. Added specifics about what constitutes a version (content fields), input/output details, default state (inactive), and the key use case (multiple versions with single template ID) to help AI agents understand the template versioning system.

data.current_summary: Create a new transactional template version.
data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to create a new version of a template.**
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new version of an existing transactional template with updated content (subject, HTML body, plain text body, etc.). Requires the template ID as a path parameter and version details in the request body. Returns the newly created template version object with a unique version ID. New versions are inactive by default; use the activate operation to set a version as active for sending. Multiple versions allow A/B testing or seasonal template variations without changing the template ID.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic. Added specifics about what constitutes a version (content fields), input/output details, default state (inactive), and the key use case (multiple versions with single template ID) to help AI agents understand the template versioning system.
